---
title: "Research Like a Software Engineer"
subtitle: "Organizing Your Projects Like Applications"
author: "Travis Hathaway"
syntax-highlighting: monokai
format: revealjs
---

## Today's agenda

::: {.incremental}
- Setting up our development environment
- An overview of the tools we'll be using
- Discovering our research question
- Creating the project structure
- Running the tools we've created
- Final analysis
:::


## Composability

As we go through today's presentation, we'll also be exploring
the power of composability...


## Windows users...

Sorry, there is literally one package (postgis) preventing us from running
this...

I highly suggest running this in **WSL** or a **Linux VM** 🐧.


## Environment management with `pixi`

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%" .incremental}
- Project based dependency management
- Uses conda packaging
- Allows to manage all dependencies, not just Python ones
- Built on top of [conda-forge](https://conda-forge.org)
- Very cool mascot 😜🪄✨
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](img/paxton-as-pixi.svg){fig-align="center"}
:::

::::

## Setup project 🚜

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone git@github.com:travishathaway/osm-workshop-2026.git

# Switch to the app/ directory
cd app/

# Start a shell with pixi
pixi shell -e dev
```

## More tools you'll love 🫶 🧰

When working working with GIS data in PostgreSQL, these two tools are indispensable:

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column .fragment width="50%"}
### pgAdmin

![](img/postgres-alt.svg)
:::

::: {.column .fragment width="50%"}
### QGIS

![](img/qgis-logo.png)
:::

::::


## Foundation 🧱

Our project will need two servers:

::: {.incremental}
1. **PostgreSQL**
    a. Holds census and OpenStreetMap (OSM) data
    b. Holds our own application data
2. **openrouteservice**
    a. Routes paths between two points (also uses OSM data)
:::

## Managing data 📊

We also use these tools for managing our data:

::: {.incremental}
1. **osmprj**
    a. Tool for importing and creating a custom schema for OSM data
2. **zensus2pgsql**
    a. Imports German Census Data into our PostgreSQL database
:::

---

Let's check out these tools...

---

## Research question 🧐

> What percentage of Berliners live within a 15 minute walk of a park/greenspace?

## What is a park/greenspace?

::: {.incremental}
- Publicly accessible
- "green" (more plants, less asphalt and concrete)
- Recreation facilities
- ???
:::

---

Time to look in our database...

---

## Every project needs a name...

::: {.incremental}

![](img/parkalyzer_logo.png)

:::

## Project structure 🏭

Some of the most important files/folders

```bash
pyproject.toml      # <-- project metadata lives here
./src/parkalyzer/   # <-- where the Python CLI lives
./scripts/          # <-- important scripts for development
                    #     environment
./tests/            # <-- tests, yes research projects need
                    #     these too!!! 😜
```

---

Let's checkout this tool! 🏃‍♂️

---


## Q/A Round 🙋‍♀️🙋‍♂️

Thanks for listening!

Happy to answer any questions you have.
